title: Bruins’ prospect James Hagens turns pro, will begin career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, a top Boston Bruins prospect, ended his collegiate career to sign a pro contract with the Providence Bruins.
Hagens posted 47 points in 34 games as a sophomore at Boston College, winning the Beanpot in February.
The 19-year-old forward, drafted seventh overall in 2025, is expected to debut against the Springfield Thunderbirds on Wednesday.

title: 7 X-factors on 2026 Red Sox roster
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox players like Marcelo Mayer, Brayan Bello, and Wilyer Abreu are key for success in the 2026 season.
Roman Anthony is expected to lead the lineup despite limited experience, following a strong performance post call-up.
Caleb Durbin replaces Alex Bregman at third base, bringing strong defense and plate discipline to the Red Sox.

title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Bradley H. Jones Jr. will not seek reelection, ending his 32-year tenure as a Massachusetts State Representative.
Jones, 61, has served as the Republican Minority Leader since 2002 and represented the 20th Middlesex District.
Jones has cast over 8,180 roll call votes and plans to serve until January 2027 before stepping down.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: No ICE agents were deployed to Logan Airport despite President Trump's order for agents at busy airports.
The TSA union president confirmed no ICE presence at New England airports but noted potential future deployment.
Logan Airport has remained operational without major delays, unlike other U.S. airports where ICE was deployed.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez is on paid leave pending an external investigation, as confirmed by the mayor.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty has been named acting chief during Suarez's absence since February 26.
Suarez took on the role of provisional chief in 2021 and became chief in 2023, with details undisclosed.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was found stabbed in the abdomen and leg near 72 Seaport Blvd early Sunday.
The victim received emergency medical assistance and was transported to a local hospital; his status is unknown.
No suspects have been identified and no arrests made; police are actively investigating the incident.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's bench trial began in Essex County Superior Court; she's charged with assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, allegedly aimed a weapon at Patrick Noonan before being shot by him in June 2025.
The defense argues Fitzsimmons intended to harm herself, not Noonan, while the prosecution claims she posed a threat to the officer.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins host Blackstone Valley Co-op hockey team at Warrior Ice Arena after winning Rhode Island Division II state championship.
Blackstone Valley's visit was part of the Bruins' effort to support the team following a tragic family loss of their captain, Colin Dorgan.
Bruins players, including Jeremy Swayman, interacted with the team, signing gear and taking photos during the practice session.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots signed offensive tackle James Hudson, entering his sixth NFL season, released by the Giants.
James Hudson played 60 career games with 19 starts, mostly at right tackle, adding depth to the Patriots' offensive line.
Hudson, a former fourth-round pick, spent four years with the Browns and played for the Giants last season.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins prospect James Hagens has yet to sign a pro contract after concluding his sophomore season at Boston College.
Hagens, a top-10 NHL Draft pick, recorded 47 points in 34 games and could transition directly to the NHL.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm is open to integrating younger players, citing experience with college athletes in pro ranks.
